title: Day 24 Quiz — Final Interview Readiness
description: Last quiz in the FrontForge React Interview Track. Quick check across all five mock interview rounds before you graduate.

questions.prompt: Round 1 JavaScript: what runs first after synchronous code finishes?

options.text: setTimeout callback (macrotask)
Promise.then callback (microtask)
requestAnimationFrame
Another synchronous block

questions.explanation: Microtasks drain before the next macrotask — core event loop question in every frontend loop.

questions.prompt: During Round 3 machine coding, using Google or AI assistants is allowed if you are stuck.
questions.explanation: Mock interview rules: no AI, no Google — official documentation only, matching real interview constraints.
call explicitly sets this — Round 1 JavaScript topic from Week 1–3 review.

questions.prompt: Round 2 React: which cause infinite re-renders? Select all that apply.

options.text: useEffect(() => setCount(c => c + 1)) with no dependency array
useState initializer as a function: useState(() => compute())
setState called unconditionally during render
useEffect with empty deps on mount only

questions.explanation: Round 5 debugging and Round 2 React — classic infinite render traps.

questions.prompt: Round 4 Project Discussion: what should you explain about the FrontForge JSON renderer?

options.text: It hardcodes one HTML page per day
Lesson blocks are typed discriminated unions rendered by a block registry
It requires a backend CMS to function
It only supports plain text paragraphs

questions.explanation: Block renderer maps JSON block types to React components — key FrontForge architecture point.

questions.prompt: Round 3 machine coding: clarify requirements, define types, then build {{blank1}} scope first. Use {{blank2}} for API errors with retry.
questions.explanation: MVP-first and error UI are non-negotiable in every machine coding round.

questions.prompt: Complete the debounce pattern for search input.
questions.explanation: Cleanup clears pending timer — prevents stale searches and memory leaks.

questions.prompt: In one sentence: what does a self-evaluation score of 3 stars mean on graduation day?
questions.sampleAnswer: Interview-ready with identifiable gaps — competent enough to start applying while targeting weak areas for improvement.
questions.explanation: Honest self-assessment drives your post-graduation study plan.

questions.prompt: Round 5 Debugging: deleting the wrong list item after reorder usually indicates?

options.text: Missing TypeScript strict mode
Using array index as key instead of stable unique id
RTK Query cache stale
CSS z-index conflict

questions.explanation: Wrong key breaks React identity tracking — debugged on Day 22 and tested again today.

questions.prompt: After graduating FrontForge, what is the recommended next step?

options.text: Wait until you feel 100% ready before applying anywhere
Start applying — treat real interviews as practice and improve continuously
Restart Week 1 from scratch
Stop coding until you receive an offer

questions.explanation: Graduation message: interviews are practice — start applying and improve from feedback.
